Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Active Directory Hacking Tool Scheduled Task Network Traffic User Account Domain Name Registrar Public IP/Network Block OS Process File Command DNS Record Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility Process Creation (Mechanism) Custom Command and Control Protocol PowerShell Script Scheduled Task/Job PowerShell Script Application Log Content (Mechanism) File Metadata (Mechanism) Command Execution (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Command Execution (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about Indian energy companies using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers monitor network traffic between nodes of a public or multicast network to capture sensitive information.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attackers create custom malware for the attack using software deployment tools.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers target specific employees with tailored phishing emails.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in company websites.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attackers establish communication channels with compromised systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attackers use PowerShell for lateral movement and privilege escalation.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks for continuous access.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers use WMI for persistence and privilege escalation.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers gain elevated privileges on compromised systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attackers hide malicious code from detection.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers modify system firmware to evade detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to crack passwords.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: The attackers steal credentials using the pass-the-hash technique.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers identify network services and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers discover running processes and services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use remote services for lateral movement.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attackers use valid accounts for lateral movement.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from local networks.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data via removable media.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate data using alternative protocols.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers use FTP for data exfiltration.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt critical infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers destroy critical data.

Scenario.summary: The attackers conduct a complex attack on the energy sector, starting with reconnaissance and resource development, then moving to initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and finally impact.
